file.description: Repo-relative path, no a/ or b/ prefix.

line.description: Line in the new file's coordinate space. 0 only for deletion anchors handled out-of-band.

rule_id.description: Static-analysis rule ID (e.g. F401, DS-002). Omit for LLM-specialist findings.

reference.description: Stable rule/advisory URL when the tool emits one.

status.description: skipped = tool legitimately absent; failed = error; ok = ran.

statusReason.description: One-line reason when status is skipped or failed.

verdict.description: Synthesiser is sole authority. COMMENT is never emitted here — only a user override produces it.

rubricRowApplied.description: Verdict-rubric row, first match wins. Omitted in local mode.

rubricReason.description: One-line condition matched, may cite [#N] tokens.

bodyText.description: The synthesiser's full prose report (markdown). Free-text — the schema wraps, never flattens, the judgement.

verdict.description: NONE in local mode (pre-review produces no verdict).

bodyText.description: Rendered GitHub body, Class D transformations already applied in code.

subjectType.description: Present only for file-level comments (no usable line). Absent for line-level comments.

log.description: Full unfiltered analytical payload for the durable log. Host writes it to disk when orchestration.full_log is true; absent on lightweight/empty bundles.

bodyText.description: The synthesiser's complete verbatim prose (all sections).

status.description: skipped = nothing to review; failed = error; ok = ran.

statusReason.description: One-line reason when status is skipped or failed.

opinionsMarkdown.description: The cross-reviewer's verbatim ## Cross-Review Opinions block (Agree/Disagree/Escalate verdicts + reasoning). Free-text — the synthesiser reads it exactly as it reads inline cross-review prose. Empty string when no opinions.

escalations.description: New cross-domain findings this reviewer raised. Each is a full finding; provenance (the cross-reviewer's own domain) is attached by review-core, not the agent.
